name: Default Template
description: Generic starter template for any website with shadcn/ui components

available_pages.name: Home
available_pages.description: Main app entry point - REPLACE this content for single-purpose apps (task manager, calculator, etc.)

section_patterns.hero: Full-width section with headline, description, and CTA buttons
section_patterns.features: Grid of 3 cards showcasing key benefits
section_patterns.testimonials: Customer quotes with avatar in card format

custom_components.name: Layout
custom_components.description: Main layout component with navigation and footer

custom_components.name: Navigation
custom_components.description: Responsive navigation with mobile menu, theme toggle, and skip link for accessibility

custom_components.name: ThemeToggle
custom_components.description: Dropdown menu for switching between light, dark, and system themes

custom_components.name: ErrorBoundary
custom_components.description: Catches React errors and shows fallback UI. Wrap pages or sections that might fail.

custom_components.name: ProtectedRoute
custom_components.description: Wraps routes requiring authentication. Redirects to login if not authenticated.

custom_components.name: LoadingState
custom_components.description: Consistent loading spinner with optional message

custom_components.name: EmptyState
custom_components.description: Shown when lists are empty - includes icon, title, description, and optional action
custom_components.usage: <EmptyState icon={<Package />} title="No products" description="Add your first product" action={{ label: 'Add Product', onClick: handleAdd }} />

utility_hooks.name: useTheme
utility_hooks.description: Theme management hook for light/dark/system mode switching with localStorage persistence
utility_hooks.usage: const { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } = useTheme(); // theme: 'light' | 'dark' | 'system', resolvedTheme: 'light' | 'dark'

utility_hooks.name: useData
utility_hooks.description: Auto-switching data hook - uses Firestore when enabled, falls back to sample data when disabled. Perfect for building features that work with or without Firebase.
utility_hooks.usage: const { data, loading, isLive, add, update, remove } = useData('products', SAMPLE_PRODUCTS);

utility_hooks.name: useFirestore
utility_hooks.description: Real-time Firestore CRUD operations with automatic collection prefixing
utility_hooks.usage: const { documents, loading, add, update, remove } = useFirestore<Product>('products');

utility_hooks.name: useAuth
utility_hooks.description: Firebase authentication hook for login, signup, and user state
utility_hooks.usage: const { user, signIn, signUp, signOut, isAuthenticated } = useAuth();

utility_hooks.name: useSystemStorage
utility_hooks.description: File upload hook for System Storage API

utility_libs.name: validation
utility_libs.description: Zod schemas for form validation with react-hook-form integration
utility_libs.usage: import { contactFormSchema } from '../lib/validation'; const form = useForm({ resolver: zodResolver(contactFormSchema) });

utility_libs.name: format
utility_libs.description: Formatting utilities for prices, dates, numbers, and strings
utility_libs.usage: import { formatPrice, formatDate } from '../lib/format'; formatPrice(99.99) → '$99.99'; formatDate(new Date()) → 'Jan 29, 2026'

theming.description: Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui theming with CSS variables for dark/light mode support
IMPORTANT_RULES: NEVER use hardcoded colors like bg-blue-600, text-blue-500 for themed elements
ALWAYS use semantic classes: bg-primary, text-primary, bg-secondary, text-foreground, etc.
For accent colors that should vary by mode, use dark: prefix: text-emerald-600 dark:text-emerald-400
For links: text-primary hover:underline NOT text-blue-600
For icon backgrounds: bg-primary/10 NOT bg-blue-50
For success states: bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-700 dark:text-emerald-400
For warning states: bg-amber-500/10 text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-400
For error states: use destructive variant or text-destructive
CTA sections can use bg-primary text-primary-foreground for inverted theme sections
For custom styles beyond theme variables, add them to src/custom.css (NOT index.css which is system-managed)

storage.description: File uploads via System Storage API
storage.usage: const { upload, deleteFile, getFiles, uploading, error } = useSystemStorage();

firebase.usage: const { documents, loading, error, add, update, remove } = useFirestore<YourType>('collection-name');

auth.usage: const { user, signIn, signUp, signOut, isAuthenticated, loading } = useAuth();

usage_examples.single_purpose_app: IMPORTANT: For requests like 'create a task manager', 'build a calculator', or 'make a todo app', REPLACE the content in src/pages/Home.tsx - do NOT create a separate page. The Home page IS the app.
usage_examples.adding_section: For 'add X' requests (e.g., 'add pricing', 'add testimonials'), add as a new section within Home.tsx
usage_examples.adding_page: Only create new pages in src/pages/ when user explicitly says 'add a page', 'create a new page', or 'separate page for X'
usage_examples.adding_route: Only add new routes when creating a new page. Add to routes array: { path: '/page', element: <Page />, showInNav: true, layout: 'default' }
usage_examples.using_layout: Set layout: 'default' in route config for pages that need navigation
usage_examples.using_storage: Import useSystemStorage from '../hooks/useSystemStorage' to enable file uploads. Call upload(file) to upload, getFiles() to list, deleteFile(id) to delete.
usage_examples.using_firebase: Import { initFirebase } from '../lib/firebase' and call it once in App.tsx. Then use useFirestore<T>('collection') hook in components for real-time CRUD operations.
usage_examples.using_auth: Import { useAuth } from '../hooks/useAuth'. Use signIn(email, password) for login, signUp(email, password, name) for registration.

prompts.title: CRITICAL: Check Project Capabilities FIRST
prompts.content:
Before creating ANY feature that involves saving data, user accounts, or file uploads:

1. Call `getProjectCapabilities` tool IMMEDIATELY
2. Check the response for `firebase.enabled` and `storage.enabled`
3. If enabled: Use the template's hooks (useFirestore, useAuth, useSystemStorage)
4. If disabled: Tell user their plan doesn't include this feature

**Keywords that REQUIRE capability check:**
save, store, persist, remember, login, signup, auth, upload, profile picture, submit form, review, comment, todo, task, note, bookmark, blog (with submissions)

**NEVER use:**
- `alert()` for user feedback - use Toast/Sonner component
- Static arrays for user-submitted data
- Local state for data that should persist

prompts.title: Firebase Data Storage ENABLED
prompts.content:
This project has Firebase/Firestore enabled for data persistence.

**MANDATORY for any data that should persist:**
- Task managers, todo lists, notes -> useFirestore
- User profiles, settings -> useFirestore
- Reviews, comments, posts -> useFirestore
- Any form that "saves" data -> useFirestore

**Before writing Firebase code:**
1. Call `getProjectCapabilities` to confirm enabled
2. Call `getFirestoreCollections` to discover existing collections
3. REUSE existing collection names - don't create duplicates

**Import:** `import { useFirestore } from '../hooks/useFirestore';`

**Usage:**
```tsx
const { documents, loading, add, update, remove } = useFirestore<MyType>('collection');
// documents auto-sync in real-time!
await add({ field: 'value' });
await update(id, { field: 'newValue' });
await remove(id);
```

prompts.title: User Authentication ENABLED
File Storage ENABLED
Dynamic Features NOT Available
prompts.content:
This project does NOT have Firebase or Storage enabled.

**If user requests features requiring:**
- Data persistence (saving tasks, notes, reviews, bookmarks)
- User accounts (login, signup, profiles)
- File uploads (images, documents)

**You MUST:**
1. Explain clearly: "Your current plan doesn't include data storage. To save data between sessions, you'll need to enable Firebase in your project settings."
2. Offer to build a DEMO version using React state (data lost on page refresh)
3. Make it clear the demo won't persist data

**Example response:**
"I can build a task manager for you! However, your plan doesn't include data storage, so tasks won't be saved when you close the browser. Would you like me to build a demo version, or would you prefer to enable Firebase first?"

prompts.title: Firebase ENABLED but Storage DISABLED
prompts.content:
**Database available but file uploads are NOT.**

You CAN:
- Save any data to Firestore (use useFirestore hook)
- Use authentication (useAuth hook)
- Real-time data sync

You CANNOT:
- Upload files or images
- Use useSystemStorage hook

**For features needing images:**
- Use placeholder URLs: `https://placehold.co/400x300`
- Accept image URLs as text input
- Use external image hosting

```tsx
<Input
  placeholder="Image URL"
  value={imageUrl}
  onChange={(e) => setImageUrl(e.target.value)}
/>
```

**Tell the user:** "Your plan includes database storage but not file uploads. You can save data and use image URLs."

prompts.title: Storage ENABLED but Firebase DISABLED
CRITICAL: Dark/Light Mode Theming
prompts.content:
This template uses Tailwind v4 with shadcn/ui CSS variables for automatic dark/light mode support.

**NEVER use hardcoded colors for themed UI elements:**
❌ bg-blue-600, text-blue-500, bg-gray-100, border-gray-200
✅ bg-primary, text-primary, bg-secondary, border-border

**Color mapping:**
- Primary actions/links: `text-primary`, `bg-primary`
- Backgrounds: `bg-background`, `bg-card`, `bg-secondary`
- Text: `text-foreground`, `text-muted-foreground`
- Borders: `border-border`, `border-input`
- Icon backgrounds: `bg-primary/10` NOT `bg-blue-50`

**For accent colors that need dark mode variants:**
```tsx
// Success states
className="bg-emerald-500/10 text-emerald-700 dark:text-emerald-400"

// Warning states
className="bg-amber-500/10 text-amber-700 dark:text-amber-400"

// Info states
className="bg-sky-500/10 text-sky-700 dark:text-sky-400"
```

**CTA/Hero sections with inverted colors:**
```tsx
<section className="bg-primary text-primary-foreground">
  <Button variant="secondary">Action</Button>
</section>
```

**Theme toggle is already in Navigation. To use programmatically:**
```tsx
import { useTheme } from '../hooks/useTheme';
const { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme } = useTheme();
```

prompts.title: Icon Usage - Lucide React
prompts.content:
This template uses lucide-react for icons.

**Import format:**
```tsx
import { Home, User, Settings, Search, Menu, X, ChevronRight, Plus, Trash2 } from 'lucide-react';
```

**Usage:**
```tsx
<Home className="h-6 w-6" />
<User className="h-5 w-5 text-gray-600" />
<Settings className="h-4 w-4 text-blue-500" />
```

**Common icons:**
- Navigation: Home, Menu, X, ChevronRight, ArrowLeft
- Actions: Plus, Minus, Trash2, Pencil, Check, X
- User: User, Users, LogIn, LogOut, Settings
- Status: CheckCircle, AlertCircle, Info, HelpCircle

prompts.title: Additional Tool Calls for Data Features
prompts.content:
**When user request involves data persistence, add these to your tool sequence:**

After `readFile("template.json")` and `readFile("src/pages/Home.tsx")`:

3. `getProjectCapabilities` - Check what features are enabled
4. If `firebase.enabled`: `getFirestoreCollections` - Discover existing data
5. Then proceed with `createPlan` or file operations

**This prevents:**
- Building static pages when Firebase is available
- Creating duplicate collections
- Using alert() when Toast is available

prompts.title: Complete Firebase Example
